a_image.alt: Grunge style mood board — Subculture · Raw & Gritty

sections.a_description: Torn edges, dirty textures, and the raw anti-aesthetic of 90s alternative culture brought to screen — Grunge design embraces everything that polished design rejects. Noise is a feature, imperfection is intentional, and the dirty yellow accents against dark, textured backgrounds evoke photocopied zines, wheat-pasted posters, and the defiant visual language of a subculture that wore its roughness as a badge of honor.

This deliberately raw style powers music venue websites, indie record label pages, skateboarding brand sites, and alternative fashion lookbooks. You'll see it on punk zine archives, tattoo studio portfolios, underground event promotions, and any digital presence that wants to signal authenticity through imperfection — where looking too polished would be the ultimate sin.

The raw, distressed aesthetic of grunge — torn edges, dirty textures, heavy contrast, and the anti-establishment energy of 90s alternative culture translated into digital form. Background: dirty off-white (#e8e2d8) with a paper-like warmth suggesting aged, stained material. The .inner panel has a dark, almost black background (#1a1612) with rough texture energy — content emerging from darkness like photocopied zines. Colors: dirty yellow (#d4a520) as the primary accent — the color of aged paper, caution tape, and grunge-era typography. Off-white (#e8e2d8) for headings, muted tan (#a09580) for body text. The aesthetic is deliberately imperfect — embracing noise, distress, and rawness as design virtues. Set in Oswald — a condensed gothic that captures the heavy, poster-like quality of grunge typography. Heading at weight 700, 28px, line-height 1.2, text-transform uppercase, color #e8e2d8. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, color #d4a520. Body at weight 300, 14px, line-height 1.6, color #a09580. Buttons: primary — dirty yellow (#d4a520), dark text (#1a1612), 0 border-radius. Secondary: transparent, yellow border. Alternatives: Bebas Neue (condensed impact), Barlow Condensed (narrow gothic).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
